doc_lines: |type|`List[float]` with exactly two elements representing the x and y components of a 2D vector.
|type|`List[float]` with exactly three elements representing the x, y, and z components of a 3D vector.
Describes the type of a camera object. Required by |func|`tde4.createCamera`.
* |value|`"SEQUENCE"` - Represents a camera that is defined by a sequence of frames.
* |value|`"REF_FRAME"` - Represents a camera that is defined by a single reference frame.
In 3DE4, spline behavior is determined by the modes of adjacent keys.
A segment uses the lowest polynomial degree that satisfies the key constraints.
* |value|`"LINEAR"`: No direct tangent control at this key. Tangent(s) are implied by the minimal-degree interpolation constraints. Two neighboring |value|`"LINEAR"` keys produce a straight segment.
* |value|`"BROKEN"`: Left and right tangents are independently editable. The function is continuous at the key, but generally not differentiable there.
* |value|`"SMOOTH"`: Left and right tangents are coupled (same direction/shape constraint) and editable as a smooth pair. Adjacent segments are typically quadratic or cubic.
Curves are extrapolated constantly outside the key range.
Therefore, even an endpoint key in |value|`"SMOOTH"` mode is generally not differentiable at the boundary.
Represents the result of a least-squares fit, containing the following fields:
|var|`position`: |type|`Vector3D_t`, the position of the fitted camera or object.
|var|`rotation`: |type|`Vector3D_t`, the rotation of the fitted camera or object as Euler angles in |term|`Rotation_order_ZXY`.
|var|`focal_length_cm`: |type|`float`, the focal length of the fitted camera in centimeters.
|var|`deviation_px`: |type|`float`, the average reprojection error of the points used for fitting, measured in pixels.
Describes the type of a point group. Required by |func|`tde4.createPGroup`.
* |value|`"CAMERA"` - Represents the point group that is used for camera solving.
Each point in this group corresponds to a 3D point in the scene that is tracked in the footage and used for solving the camera.
There can be at most one point group of this type in a project.
* |value|`"OBJECT"` - Represents the point group that is used for object tracking.
Each point in this group corresponds to a 3D point on the object that is tracked in the footage and used for solving the object's motion.
* |value|`"MOCAP"` - Represents the point group that is used for motion capture.
Each point in this group corresponds to a marker on the actor or object that is tracked in the footage and used for motion capture.
Describes the status of a 3D point. Required by |func|`tde4.getPoint3DStatus`.
* |value|`"NOT_CALCULATED"` - The point's 3D position has not been calculated yet. This is the initial status of a point when it is created.
* |value|`"CALCULATED"` - The point's 3D position has been fully calculated.
* |value|`"TRIANGULATED"` - The point's 3D position has been triangulated without
affecting the camera solve. This status can occur for points with low parallax.
Required by |func|`tde4.setPointCalcMode`. This mode describes how the 3D position of a point is calculated.
* |value|`"CALC_PASSIVE"` - The point is calculated passively, i.e. it has no effect on the camera solve,
but is calculated based on the current camera parameters and other points.
* |value|`"CALC_ACTIVE"` - The point is calculated actively, i.e. it has an effect on the camera solve.
Postfilter modes for camera paths. Required by |func|`tde4.setCameraPathPostfilterMode`. Possible values are:
* |value|`"POSTFILTER_OFF"` - No postfiltering is applied to the camera path.
* |value|`"POSTFILTER_SMOOTHING"` - A smoothing filter is applied to the camera path. Results are smooth but may deviate from the original path.
* |value|`"POSTFILTER_SMOOTHING_FOURIER"` - A combined smoothing and Fourier filter is applied to the camera path.
This filter can effectively reduce jitter while preserving the overall shape of the camera path, but may introduce some overshoot in certain cases.
* |value|`"POSTFILTER_BUTTERWORTH"` - A Butterworth filter is applied to the camera path. This filter
is particularly effective at reducing high-frequency jitter in the camera path while preserving
Describes where a custom requester is currently posted.
* |value|`"REQUESTER_POSTED_IN_WINDOW"` requester is posted in its own non-modal window.
* |value|`"REQUESTER_POSTED_IN_PANE_STRUCTURE"` requester is embedded in a pane structure.
* |value|`"REQUESTER_POSTED_IN_ATTRIBUTE_EDITOR"` requester is embedded in the attribute editor.
Literal type that represents tag keys supported in script headers:
* |value|`"3DE4.script.name"` - Human-readable script name shown in 3DE4 GUI menus.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.addlabel"` - Additional label text that provides more context for invocation. The active label can be queried via |func|`tde4.getLastScriptMenuLabel`.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.version"` - Version string shown in 3DE4 GUI next to the script name.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.hide"` - If set to |value|`"true"`, the script does not appear in 3DE4 GUI menus.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.startup"` - If set to |value|`"true"`, 3DE4 executes the script during startup.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.gui.config_menus"` - If set to |value|`"true"`, the script is listed in configuration menus.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.gui.attribute_editor"` - If set to |value|`"true"`, the script is embedded in the attribute editor.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.gui.attribute_editor_pos"` - Specifies the position of the script in the attribute editor.
Human-readable script name shown in 3DE4 GUI menus.
Additional label text that provides more context for invocation.
The active label can be queried via |func|`tde4.getLastScriptMenuLabel`.
Version string shown in 3DE4 GUI next to the script name.
This tag may appear multiple times; values are concatenated with newlines.

Attribute-editor tab label; currently observed as |value|`"Camera"` plus custom labels.
Describes the tracking status of a 2D point.
* |value|`"POINT_KEYFRAME"` - The point is a keyframe, i.e. it is manually tracked and serves as a reference for interpolation.
* |value|`"POINT_KEYFRAME_END"` - The point is a keyframe that marks the end of a tracking sequence.
It is manually tracked and serves as a reference for interpolation, but also indicates that the tracking sequence ends at this point.
* |value|`"POINT_OBSOLETE"` - The point is obsolete because tracking parameters such as the search region have changed since the point was last tracked.
The point needs to be re-tracked to get an updated position.
* |value|`"POINT_INTERPOLATED"` - The point's position is interpolated based on surrounding keyframes.
This type describes the tracking status of a 2D point, similar to |type|`PointStatus2D_t`,
with an additional undefined status. Possible values are:
Describes the orientation of the stereo camera setup,
i.e. which camera is on the left and which camera is on the right.
* |value|`"STEREO_IO_STATIC"` - The interocular distance is constant and does not change over time.
* |value|`"STEREO_IO_DYNAMIC"` - The interocular distance is dynamic and explicitly time-dependent.
Describes the stereo mode of a camera. Required by |func|`tde4.setCameraStereoMode`.
The primary camera path is solved first and the secondary camera path is solved
afterwards based on the primary camera path. Possible values are:
* |value|`"STEREO_OFF"` - The camera is not in stereo mode.
* |value|`"STEREO_PRIMARY"` - The camera is the primary camera in a stereo setup.
* |value|`"STEREO_SECONDARY"` - The camera is the secondary camera in a stereo setup.
This type describes whether or not a point is surveyed, and if so,
how its surveyed position is interpreted during camera solving.
* |value|`"SURVEY_FREE"` - The point position is not pre-defined and is estimated during camera solving.
* |value|`"SURVEY_APPROX"` - The point position is approximately known and is used to guide the camera solving process.
* |value|`"SURVEY_EXACT"` - The point position is exactly known and is used as a fixed reference during camera solving.
* |value|`"SURVEY_LINEUP"` - The point has a 3D position and is used for lineup purposes; it is not used for camera solving.
* |value|`"TRACKING_MARKER"` - Track a marker, i.e. an object with two well-defined color zones,
such as a circular marker with a ring in one color and an inner circle in another color.
Other shapes with similar color zoning can also be tracked as markers, as long as it is possible
to estimate a center position based on the color zoning. Image_controls can be used to enhance
the color zoning of markers and improve tracking stability.
This type is used by multiple functions, e.g. |func|`tde4.createCustomRequester`, |func|`tde4.setWidgetLinks`, and |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets`.
It describes how a widget is attached to its parent widget, window, or another widget.
Offsets and percentage positions are interpreted in |term|`GUI_coordinates`. Possible values are:
* |value|`"ATTACH_WIDGET"` - Attach to the specified widget. |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets` expects an offset in pixels from the specified widget.
* |value|`"ATTACH_OPPOSITE_WIDGET"` - Attach to the opposite side of the specified widget. |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets` expects an offset in pixels from the opposite side of the specified widget.
* |value|`"ATTACH_WINDOW"` - Attach to the parent window. |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets` expects an offset in pixels from the parent window.
* |value|`"ATTACH_OPPOSITE_WINDOW"` - Attach to the opposite side of the parent window. |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets` expects an offset in pixels from the opposite side of the parent window.
* |value|`"ATTACH_POSITION"` - Specify the position in percentage relative to the parent.
If this mode is used, the position is determined by the offsets set by |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets`
and is interpreted as percentage of the parent widget or window's size along the corresponding GUI axis, rather than absolute pixel offsets.
* |value|`"ATTACH_NONE"` - Do not attach. Missing positions are computed based on the current position and default size of the widget.
* |value|`"ATTACH_AS_IS"` - Keep the current attachment. This allows to modify some of the widget's attachments while keeping the others unchanged.
This type defines horizontal text alignment for label widgets.
* |value|`"ALIGN_LABEL_LEFT"` - Align label text to the left.
* |value|`"ALIGN_LABEL_RIGHT"` - Align label text to the right.
|type|`Callable[[RequesterID_t, WidgetName_t, WidgetCallbackActionEnum], None]` - Standard widget callback function type. The callback receives the requester ID, widget name,
and a high-level widget action code.

|type|`Callable[[RequesterID_t, WidgetName_t, int, int, WidgetCallbackRawEventEnum, WidgetCallbackModKeysEnum, int], None]` - Raw event callback function type for widgets. The callback receives the requester ID, widget name, mouse position |var|`(x, y)`,
raw event type, modifier keys, and an event-dependent raw payload value.
tde4.WidgetRawEventCallbackFunction_t.annotation: Callable[[str, str, int, int, tde4.WidgetCallbackRawEventEnum, tde4.WidgetCallbackModKeysEnum, int], NoneType]

Length units: Physical length units in the API are always in centimeters.
This includes, for example, focal length, sensor size ("Filmback"), 3D point positions, etc.

text: For a given raster image, |dfn|`Image_units` refers to
the coordinate system of the image, where (0,0) denotes the lower left corner of the lower left pixel
of the image, and (1,1) denotes the upper right corner of the upper right pixel of the image.
The Field of View, the rectangular area in the image that represents the camera's nominal filmback.
In 3DE4's GUI this is marked by dashed white lines.
The coordinate system spanned by the Field of View.
(0,0) denotes the lower left corner of the Field of View,
and (1,1) denotes the upper right corner of the Field of View.
The coordinate system used for the OpenGL projection and rendering pipeline,
OpenGL normalized device coordinates,
where the visible area is defined by the frustum from |value|`(-1,-1,-1)` to |value|`(1,1,1)`.
Any triple of Euler angles |var|`phi_x`, |var|`phi_y`, |var|`phi_z` is interpreted
in |dfn|`Rotation_order_ZXY`, if and only if the following conditions are fulfilled:

* The corresponding rotation matrix |var|`R` (or unit quaternion) results from consecutive application of rotation around Z-axis, followed by rotation around X-axis, followed by rotation around Y-axis, like so for any vector |var|`v` in 3d-space: |lit|`v -> R v = R_y R_x R_z v`.

* Rotations are right-handed, i.e. for a positive angle |var|`phi` and a rotation axis |var|`a`, the cross product of any vector |var|`v` (not parallel to |var|`a`) with its derivative at |lit|`phi=0` points in the direction of |var|`a`: |lit|`dot(wdg( v,(d/dphi R(phi)|phi=0 v) ),a) > 0`, where |lit|`wdg` denotes the vector- or cross-product.
In (right-handed) Cartesian coordinates, this means a positive rotation around the
Z-axis maps the positive X-axis towards the positive Y-axis:
A widget is considered |dfn|`sensitive` if it is enabled and can be interacted with by the user.
A non-|dfn|`sensitive` widget is typically grayed out and does not respond to user interaction.
The coordinate system used for GUI elements, where the origin |value|`(0,0)` is at
the upper left corner of the parent widget or window, and coordinates are in pixels.
A |dfn|`GUI_color_index` maps point color indices used by 3DE4's GUI to fixed colors:
|value|`0=red`, |value|`1=green`, |value|`2=blue`, |value|`3=yellow`,
|value|`4=black`, |value|`5=white`, |value|`6=gray`, |value|`7=purple`,
|value|`8=cyan`, |value|`9=light red`, |value|`10=light green`, |value|`11=light blue`.
A unique integer identifier for an object that remains constant across sessions
and is used to reference the object in scripts and the GUI.
The |dfn|`Matrix_tool` is available in 3DE4's |term|`Distortion Grid Controls` (|key|`F3`).
It supports semi-automatic detection of checkerboard grid-shot vertices and uses them for lens-distortion calibration.
The Python API provides functions to get and set the |term|`Matrix_tool` grid dimensions, point positions, and point status.
Matrix points are addressed by their row and column indices in the grid.
The grid center point has indices |value|`(1000,1000)`.
The vertical shift of the secondary camera with respect to the primary camera in a stereo setup.
This is required for stereo rigs using semi-transparent mirrors, where the secondary camera
is physically above the primary camera and looks downwards at the scene through the mirror.
The |dfn|`Stereo_vertical_shift` models the relative position of the secondary camera
as if the mirror was not present, i.e. it is the vertical distance between the two cameras
as if they were both positioned side to side, looking straight ahead without the semi-transparent mirror.
The |dfn|`Stereo_depth_shift` models the relative forward/backward offset of the secondary camera
with respect to the primary camera in a stereo setup.
The sign convention follows 3DE4's standard coordinate system
(|value|`X=right`, |value|`Y=up`, |value|`Z=back`).
Therefore, a negative |term|`Stereo_depth_shift` means the secondary camera is located further forward
than the primary camera.
The |dfn|`Stereo_rotation_policy` controls how the secondary stereo camera is rotated relative
to the primary camera while both are oriented towards a convergence point.
In the ideal rig case, a pure Y-axis relative rotation is sufficient.
Allowing full XYZ relative rotation can model mechanical rig imperfections.
Together with |term|`Stereo_vertical_shift` and |term|`Stereo_depth_shift`, this may require
image consolidation/correction passes (for example via |func|`tde4.calcHomography2D`).
The |dfn|`Selection_flag` is a boolean attribute of various objects in 3DE4, such as cameras, 3D points, etc.
Selection flags are used to mark objects for selection in the GUI, and can be set and queried via the Python API.
The effect of setting/unsetting a |term|`Selection_flag` is equivalent to selecting/unselecting the corresponding
object in the GUI by mouse-clicking on it.
The |dfn|`Hide_flag` is a boolean attribute of points in 3DE4.
Hidden points are not displayed in the viewport. There is a separate hide flag
which controls whether a point is visible in the timeline or not.
The |dfn|`Widget_default_positioning` describes the automatic layout used by a custom requester
when no explicit widget layout is defined. Widgets are placed vertically (one below another)
in insertion order. For menu widgets, a menubar forms a horizontal bar and menus are arranged
within that bar in insertion order.
The |dfn|`Rootframes` of a solve are a pair of frames used to estimate
the initial camera pose and point cloud.
|dfn|`Rootframes` are typically chosen as two frames with strong parallax
and a sufficient number of tracked points.
They can be set manually in the GUI or determined automatically by 3DE4's core.
The |dfn|`Display_Area` is the rectangular viewport region where footage and 3D elements are shown.
Depending on the active workspace tab, it hosts one of:
* |term|`Overview_Controls` (|key|`F1`)
* |term|`Manual_Tracking_Controls` (|key|`F2`)
* |term|`Distortion_Grid_Controls` (|key|`F3`)
* |term|`Autotracking_Controls` (|key|`F4`)
* |term|`Lineup_Controls` (|key|`F5`)
* |term|`Orientation_Controls` (|key|`F6`)
The |dfn|`Overview_Controls` (|key|`F1`) is a panel in 3DE4's GUI that provides a view to the footage
, point tracks, and 3D objects through the solved camera.
Images are cached and down-scaled for fast scrubbing, and can be rendered with or without lens distortion.
Tools include rendering frames or depth maps to the filesystem.
The |dfn|`Manual_Tracking_Controls` (|key|`F2`) is a panel in 3DE4's GUI for manual tracking of points on footage.
Tools include adding, deleting, and moving tracks, as well as adjusting track properties and tracking status.
The |dfn|`Distortion_Grid_Controls` (|key|`F3`) is a panel in 3DE4's GUI for lens distortion calibration using grid shots.
Tools include the |term|`Matrix_tool` for semi-automatic detection of checkerboard grid vertices,
handling dynamic distortion (focal-length and/or focus-driven),
applying and removing lens distortion,
converting ST-maps to lens distortion parameter sets,
reparameterization of distortion parameters due to changes in filmback or focal length,
and specialized tools for anamorphic lens distortion.
The |dfn|`Autotracking_Controls` (|key|`F4`) is a panel in 3DE4's GUI for automatic tracking of points on footage.
Tools include editing masks to exclude areas from tracking.
The |dfn|`Lineup_Controls` (|key|`F5`) is a panel in 3DE4's GUI for manually
aligning 3D models to footage by adjusting camera position/orientation and
model position/orientation/scale.
It also provides tools for extracting vertex positions from OBJ files and
assigning them to points in 3DE4.
The |dfn|`Orientation_Controls` (|key|`F6`) is a panel in 3DE4's GUI for rendering the scene in 3D.
It provides tools for adjusting the point group's position and orientation in 3D space, and for
interacting with 3D elements in the scene.
The |dfn|`Image_Controls` is a panel in 3DE4's GUI for adjusting the display of footage during 2D tracking.
It provides controls for adjusting the brightness, contrast, and gamma of the footage,
and allows live painting of masks that can be used to exclude areas from tracking.
The |dfn|`Deviation_Browser` is a panel in 3DE4's GUI that shows the reprojection
error of 3D points on a per-point, per-frame basis as well as averaged over all frames,
for the current camera and point group. In detail, the following deviations are shown:
* The deviation of the pointgroup, averaged over all points and frames.
* The deviation of each point, averaged over all frames.
* The deviation of the pointgroup in each frame, averaged over all points.
* The deviation of each point in each frame.
It allows users to assess the overall quality of the camera solve,
identify points and frames with high reprojection error, and adjust
the properties of individual points in those frames.
If points have dynamic weighting, the |term|`Deviation_Browser` also shows the point weight curve
and provides tools to adjust the weights of points over time.
The |dfn|`Timeline_Editor` is a panel in 3DE4's GUI that shows all points
or subsets of points in a project as rows, and frames as columns. It allows
users to see and edit the tracking status of points over time, and to shift point tracking data in time.
The |dfn|`Parameter_Adjustment` is a panel/window in 3DE4's GUI that provides controls
for adjusting various parameters of the camera solve. The panel is subdivided
in three sections: the first section is a representation of the target function (deviation in pixel)
versus the parameters being adjusted. Up to three parameters can be selected for adjustment,
and the target function is plotted as a curve in 2D for one parameter, as a surface in 3D for two parameters,
or as a coloured point cloud for three parameters. If more than three parameters are selected,
only the first three are plotted, but all are adjusted in the same optimization loop.
The second section is a list of parameters selected for adjustment,
with controls for adjusting their values, ranges and adjustment methods.
The third section provides advanced controls for network calculation (multi-host processing,
see |ref|`Network Calculation Manual <https://3dequalizer.com/index.php?site=tech_docs&id=140409_01&q=network>`),
and for including or excluding parts of the scene from adjustment/solving.
A |dfn|`Scripting_tag` is a metadata header entry in a Python script comment block
(e.g. |value|`# 3DE4.script.gui: ...`) used by 3DE4 to classify and place scripts in GUI menus,
button areas, startup behavior, and related integration points.
|dfn|`Synchronization` in 3DE4 defines how multiple cameras are temporally aligned
and constrained relative to each other in shared solve workflows.
It combines:
* a sync role per camera (off, primary, secondary),
* optional sync constraints (none, position-fixed, rotation+position-fixed),
* a sub-frame time offset in frame units.
Typical use cases are:
* Mocap setups with several synchronized cameras,
* main/hero camera plus witness/reference cameras that should stay time-aligned.
In Python, this concept is controlled by |func|`tde4.getCameraSyncMode`,
|func|`tde4.getCameraSyncConstraints`, and |func|`tde4.getCameraSyncTimeshift`
(and their corresponding setters).
The layout scheme for matrices. Any matrix (2D or 3D) |var|`M` stored as a list of lists of |type|`float`
is laid out such that |var|`M[i]` is the |var|`i`-th row of the matrix (with 0-based indices).
|var|`M[i][j]` is the |var|`j`-th component of the |var|`i`-th row (|var|`j` is also 0-based).
This matches common mathematical notation, where for matrices |var|`M` and |var|`N`, their product |var|`P`
is defined by |var|`P[i][j]` = sum over |var|`k` of |var|`M[i][k]` * |var|`N[k][j]`.
Any camera or pointgroup motion path is subject to postfiltering, which smooths
the motion path and can help to reduce noise and jitter in the camera solve.
Postfiltering is applied after the initial solve and optimization steps, and can be adjusted by the user.
The |dfn|`Raw_curve` is part of the motion-path pipeline for cameras and point groups in 3DE4,
which consists of |term|`Raw_curve`, |term|`Edit_curve`, and |term|`Filtered_curve`.
The |term|`Raw_curve` is the initial motion path estimated by 3DE4 core algorithms before postfiltering.
During a solve-from-scratch workflow, 3DE4 generates the |term|`Raw_curve` from tracked points and the selected |term|`Rootframes`.
The |term|`Raw_curve` is then copied to the |term|`Edit_curve`; postfiltering the |term|`Edit_curve` yields the |term|`Filtered_curve`.
After solve-from-scratch, users may edit the |term|`Edit_curve` manually and reapply postfiltering to update the |term|`Filtered_curve`.
For each camera or point group there is one curve per degree of freedom: three translation curves and three rotation curves.
The |dfn|`Edit_curve` is the editable stage in the motion-path pipeline
(|term|`Raw_curve` -> |term|`Edit_curve` -> |term|`Filtered_curve`).
See |term|`Raw_curve` for the overall pipeline.
For object and mocap point groups, two representations of the |term|`Edit_curve` exist:
one in camera-local coordinates (often harder to interpret) and one in global coordinates.
The |dfn|`Filtered_curve` is the postfiltered stage in the motion-path pipeline
(|term|`Raw_curve` -> |term|`Edit_curve` -> |term|`Filtered_curve`).
It is derived from the |term|`Edit_curve` by applying the current postfilter settings.
See |term|`Raw_curve` for the overall pipeline.
|dfn|`Survey` describes user-provided 3D point constraints that guide solving.
Per point, 3DE distinguishes survey modes |value|`"SURVEY_FREE"`, |value|`"SURVEY_APPROX"`,
|value|`"SURVEY_EXACT"`, and |value|`"SURVEY_LINEUP"`.
This mode set is described by |type|`SurveyMode_t`.
In |value|`"SURVEY_FREE"`, no fixed survey constraint is applied.
In |value|`"SURVEY_APPROX"`, a soft/approximate |term|`Position_constraint` is used.
In |value|`"SURVEY_EXACT"`, the survey position is treated as an exact reference.
|dfn|`Survey_position` is the user-provided 3D point position used as |term|`Survey` input to solving.
For Camera and Object point groups, |term|`Survey_positions` are static (not frame-dependent).
For Mocap point groups, |term|`Survey_positions` may vary per frame and per camera sample.
|dfn|`Calc_position` is the computed 3D point position produced by solving.
For Camera and Object point groups, |term|`Calc_positions` are static (not frame-dependent).
For Mocap point groups, |term|`Calc_positions` may vary per frame and per camera sample.
|term|`Survey_position` and |term|`Calc_position` form an input/output concept pair for point solving APIs.
|dfn|`Calculated3D_flag` is the legacy boolean state used by |func|`tde4.isPointCalculated3D`
and |func|`tde4.setPointCalculated3D`.
|value|`0` means not calculated; any non-zero value means calculated.
|dfn|`Calculated3D_status` is the precise tri-state used by |func|`tde4.getPointCalculated3DStatus`
and |func|`tde4.setPointCalculated3DStatus`.
Possible values are |value|`"NOT_CALCULATED"`, |value|`"CALCULATED"`, and |value|`"TRIANGULATED"`,
represented by the type |type|`Point3DStatus_t`.
The |dfn|`Position_weight_blending` feature allows users to specify a blending of point weights
based on the position of the point in the camera's field of view. This can be used to,
for example, avoid discontinuities in the solved camera motion when points enter or leave
the field of view, by smoothly blending their weights in and out based on their position.
|dfn|`Position_weight_blending` is specified as a horizontal and vertical blending zone in |term|`FOV_units`.
On the FOV edges, the weight factor resulting from |term|`Position_weight_blending` is 0,
and it increases towards the center of the FOV by a cubic spline, where it reaches a value of 1 (no blending).
The idea of timeline-weight blending is analogous to |term|`Position_weight_blending`,
but instead of blending based on the position of points in the FOV, it blends based on
contiguous tracking segments in the timeline. This can be used to smoothly blend points
in and out over time, for example to avoid discontinuities when points start or stop being tracked.
The |dfn|`Film_aspect_ratio` (or short "Film Aspect" in 3DE4) is the aspect ratio of the camera's filmback,
defined as the width of the filmback divided by its height.
The |dfn|`Pixel_aspect_ratio` of a raster-based input or output device
expresses the change in aspect ratio when recording or displaying
a rectangular, aligned object as a result of non-square pixels.
Without lens distortion, it is defined as filmback aspect ratio divided by
the aspect ratio of the captured image in pixels (the definition used in 3DE4).
For most lens distortion types, |dfn|`Pixel_aspect_ratio` can be defined
within an infinitesimal neighborhood of the lens center. However, for anamorphic lenses
with lens rotation, it is not possible to express the |dfn|`Pixel_aspect_ratio` by
a simple number. These problems are of minor importance for typical use cases, since |dfn|`Pixel_aspect_ratio`
is considered as a "backend" parameter, not an input for computations.
The |dfn|`Lens_center_offset` is a 2D vector in centimeters representing the offset
of the lens center (also known as the principal point) from the nominal center of the Field of View.
The default |term|`Lens_center_offset` is (0,0), which means the lens center coincides with the nominal FOV center.
Positive values of |term|`Lens_center_offset` shift the lens center to the right and upwards,
while negative values shift it to the left and downwards.
The lens center is the fixed point of the lens distortion,
and it is also the point around which focal length is defined.
The |dfn|`Lens_2DLUT` is a two-dimensional lookup table for adjustable lens-distortion parameters
used in dynamic distortion mode |value|`"DISTORTION_DYNAMIC_FOCAL_AND_FOCUS"`.
Its two input axes are focal length and focus distance; each table sample stores one parameter value.
Calibration of a robust |term|`Lens_2DLUT` usually requires many high-quality grid-shot measurements.
In production, this mode is used less often than workflows with explicit per-frame parameter curves.
|dfn|`Model_topology` describes how a 3D model is built from vertex, line, and face index structures.
It specifies connectivity and polygon/line composition, independent of shading or texture content.
A |dfn|`Face_corner` is one corner entry inside a face's ordered vertex list.
It is addressed by a face-local index (for example |var|`vertex_index` in UV APIs), not by global vertex index.
A |dfn|`UV_coordinate` is a 2D texture coordinate pair |value|`[u, v]` assigned to a specific |term|`Face_corner`.
A face therefore carries one |term|`UV_coordinate` per corner entry.
|dfn|`Packed_RGBA32` is a packed unsigned 32-bit integer representation of color channels
(red, green, blue, alpha) used by list-based per-vertex color APIs.
Informative example in logical |value|`0xRRGGBBAA` notation:
|value|`0x7f00ff00` means |value|`R=0x7f`, |value|`G=0x00`, |value|`B=0xff`, |value|`A=0x00`.
For exact channel-level edits, prefer |func|`tde4.set3DModelVertexColor`.
The list-based APIs expose 3DE4's internal packed DB values directly.
A |dfn|`Position_constraint` is a geometric constraint object used to control position/transform relationships during solving.
|dfn|`Position_constraints` can help to align the solved result to scene geometry.
Each |term|`Position_constraint` has a specific type that defines the geometric constraint,
and it may also have an orientation that defines the direction of the constraint.
The type and orientation of a |term|`Position_constraint` are defined by the types
|type|`PosConstrMode_t` and |type|`PosConstrOrient_t`, respectively.
A |dfn|`Distance_constraint` binds two points to a target distance value.
|dfn|`Distance_constraints` are owned by a point group and are evaluated by the solver
as geometric distance conditions between the referenced point pair.
In |term|`Survey`-free solves, |dfn|`Distance_constraints` are typically used to
make the scene scale uniquely determined.
The referenced points are managed via |func|`tde4.getDConstrPoints`/|func|`tde4.setDConstrPoints`,
and the target distance is managed via |func|`tde4.getDConstrDistance`/|func|`tde4.setDConstrDistance`.
|label|`GUI behavior (informative):`
Some GUI workflows may restrict assigning one point to multiple |term|`Distance_constraint`s.
The Python API and solver core may still accept and evaluate such setups.
A |dfn|`Camera_mask` is a user-defined mask that can be applied to footage to exclude areas from tracking.
Masks can be image-based (black-and-white raster images) or geometry-based (splines).
Spline masks are regions specified by a set of control vertices with tangent handles.

general: MUST mirror the signatures of 3DE4's python extension for selected functions.
MUST provide type aliases for each non-built-in type used in a function signature.

Vector2D_t: |type|`List[float]` with exactly two elements representing the x and y components of a 2D vector.
Vector3D_t: |type|`List[float]` with exactly three elements representing the x, y, and z components of a 3D vector.
CameraType_t: Describes the type of a camera object. Required by |func|`tde4.createCamera`.
* |value|`"SEQUENCE"` - Represents a camera that is defined by a sequence of frames.
* |value|`"REF_FRAME"` - Represents a camera that is defined by a single reference frame.
CurveKeyMode_t: In 3DE4, spline behavior is determined by the modes of adjacent keys.
A segment uses the lowest polynomial degree that satisfies the key constraints.
* |value|`"LINEAR"`: No direct tangent control at this key. Tangent(s) are implied by the minimal-degree interpolation constraints. Two neighboring |value|`"LINEAR"` keys produce a straight segment.
* |value|`"BROKEN"`: Left and right tangents are independently editable. The function is continuous at the key, but generally not differentiable there.
* |value|`"SMOOTH"`: Left and right tangents are coupled (same direction/shape constraint) and editable as a smooth pair. Adjacent segments are typically quadratic or cubic.
Curves are extrapolated constantly outside the key range.
Therefore, even an endpoint key in |value|`"SMOOTH"` mode is generally not differentiable at the boundary.
LSFResult_t: Represents the result of a least-squares fit, containing the following fields:
|var|`position`: |type|`Vector3D_t`, the position of the fitted camera or object.
|var|`rotation`: |type|`Vector3D_t`, the rotation of the fitted camera or object as Euler angles in |term|`Rotation_order_ZXY`.
|var|`focal_length_cm`: |type|`float`, the focal length of the fitted camera in centimeters.
|var|`deviation_px`: |type|`float`, the average reprojection error of the points used for fitting, measured in pixels.
PGroupType_t: Describes the type of a point group. Required by |func|`tde4.createPGroup`.
* |value|`"CAMERA"` - Represents the point group that is used for camera solving.
Each point in this group corresponds to a 3D point in the scene that is tracked in the footage and used for solving the camera.
There can be at most one point group of this type in a project.
* |value|`"OBJECT"` - Represents the point group that is used for object tracking.
Each point in this group corresponds to a 3D point on the object that is tracked in the footage and used for solving the object's motion.
* |value|`"MOCAP"` - Represents the point group that is used for motion capture.
Each point in this group corresponds to a marker on the actor or object that is tracked in the footage and used for motion capture.
Point3DStatus_t: Describes the status of a 3D point. Required by |func|`tde4.getPoint3DStatus`.
* |value|`"NOT_CALCULATED"` - The point's 3D position has not been calculated yet. This is the initial status of a point when it is created.
* |value|`"CALCULATED"` - The point's 3D position has been fully calculated.
* |value|`"TRIANGULATED"` - The point's 3D position has been triangulated without
affecting the camera solve. This status can occur for points with low parallax.
PointCalcMode_t: Required by |func|`tde4.setPointCalcMode`. This mode describes how the 3D position of a point is calculated.
* |value|`"CALC_PASSIVE"` - The point is calculated passively, i.e. it has no effect on the camera solve,
but is calculated based on the current camera parameters and other points.
* |value|`"CALC_ACTIVE"` - The point is calculated actively, i.e. it has an effect on the camera solve.
PostfilterMode_t: Postfilter modes for camera paths. Required by |func|`tde4.setCameraPathPostfilterMode`. Possible values are:
* |value|`"POSTFILTER_OFF"` - No postfiltering is applied to the camera path.
* |value|`"POSTFILTER_SMOOTHING"` - A smoothing filter is applied to the camera path. Results are smooth but may deviate from the original path.
* |value|`"POSTFILTER_SMOOTHING_FOURIER"` - A combined smoothing and Fourier filter is applied to the camera path.
This filter can effectively reduce jitter while preserving the overall shape of the camera path, but may introduce some overshoot in certain cases.
* |value|`"POSTFILTER_BUTTERWORTH"` - A Butterworth filter is applied to the camera path. This filter
is particularly effective at reducing high-frequency jitter in the camera path while preserving
RequesterPostState_t: Describes where a custom requester is currently posted.
* |value|`"REQUESTER_POSTED_IN_WINDOW"` requester is posted in its own non-modal window.
* |value|`"REQUESTER_POSTED_IN_PANE_STRUCTURE"` requester is embedded in a pane structure.
* |value|`"REQUESTER_POSTED_IN_ATTRIBUTE_EDITOR"` requester is embedded in the attribute editor.
ScriptingTag_t: Literal type that represents tag keys supported in script headers:
* |value|`"3DE4.script.name"` - Human-readable script name shown in 3DE4 GUI menus.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.addlabel"` - Additional label text that provides more context for invocation. The active label can be queried via |func|`tde4.getLastScriptMenuLabel`.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.version"` - Version string shown in 3DE4 GUI next to the script name.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.hide"` - If set to |value|`"true"`, the script does not appear in 3DE4 GUI menus.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.startup"` - If set to |value|`"true"`, 3DE4 executes the script during startup.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.gui.config_menus"` - If set to |value|`"true"`, the script is listed in configuration menus.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.gui.attribute_editor"` - If set to |value|`"true"`, the script is embedded in the attribute editor.
* |value|`"3DE4.script.gui.attribute_editor_pos"` - Specifies the position of the script in the attribute editor.
Script_Name_t: Human-readable script name shown in 3DE4 GUI menus.
Script_AddLabel_t: Additional label text that provides more context for invocation.
The active label can be queried via |func|`tde4.getLastScriptMenuLabel`.
Script_Version_t: Version string shown in 3DE4 GUI next to the script name.
Script_Comment_t: This tag may appear multiple times; values are concatenated with newlines.
Script_GUI_Root_t: Predefined top-level GUI roots, for example |value|`"Main Window"` or |value|`"Curve Editor"`.
Script_GUI_PredefinedPath_t: Known predefined GUI menu paths, collected from bundled 3DE4 scripts.
Script_GUI_t: Accepts predefined paths and custom menu paths.
Script_GUI_Button_Align_t: Allowed alignment literals for button-form A:
Script_GUI_Button_Offset_t: Allowed offset-alignment literals for button-form B:
Script_GUI_Button_A_t: Button tag tuple form A: |type|`tuple[str, Script_GUI_Button_Align_t, int, int]`.
Script_GUI_Button_B_t: Button tag tuple form B: |type|`tuple[str, Script_GUI_Button_Offset_t, int, int, int, int]`.
Script_Hide_t: If set to |value|`"true"`, the script does not appear in 3DE4 GUI menus,
but it can still be called from other scripts or the command line.
Script_Startup_t: If set to |value|`"true"`, 3DE4 executes the script during startup.
Script_GUI_Config_Menus_t: If set to |value|`"true"`, the script is listed in configuration menus
Script_GUI_Attribute_Editor_t: Attribute-editor tab label; currently observed as |value|`"Camera"` plus custom labels.
PointStatus2D_t: Describes the tracking status of a 2D point.
* |value|`"POINT_KEYFRAME"` - The point is a keyframe, i.e. it is manually tracked and serves as a reference for interpolation.
* |value|`"POINT_KEYFRAME_END"` - The point is a keyframe that marks the end of a tracking sequence.
It is manually tracked and serves as a reference for interpolation, but also indicates that the tracking sequence ends at this point.
* |value|`"POINT_OBSOLETE"` - The point is obsolete because tracking parameters such as the search region have changed since the point was last tracked.
The point needs to be re-tracked to get an updated position.
* |value|`"POINT_INTERPOLATED"` - The point's position is interpolated based on surrounding keyframes.
PointStatus2DWithUndef_t: This type describes the tracking status of a 2D point, similar to |type|`PointStatus2D_t`,
with an additional undefined status. Possible values are:
StereoOrientation_t: Describes the orientation of the stereo camera setup,
i.e. which camera is on the left and which camera is on the right.
StereoInterocular_t: * |value|`"STEREO_IO_STATIC"` - The interocular distance is constant and does not change over time.
* |value|`"STEREO_IO_DYNAMIC"` - The interocular distance is dynamic and explicitly time-dependent.
StereoMode_t: Describes the stereo mode of a camera. Required by |func|`tde4.setCameraStereoMode`.
The primary camera path is solved first and the secondary camera path is solved
afterwards based on the primary camera path. Possible values are:
* |value|`"STEREO_OFF"` - The camera is not in stereo mode.
* |value|`"STEREO_PRIMARY"` - The camera is the primary camera in a stereo setup.
* |value|`"STEREO_SECONDARY"` - The camera is the secondary camera in a stereo setup.
SurveyMode_t: This type describes whether or not a point is surveyed, and if so,
how its surveyed position is interpreted during camera solving.
* |value|`"SURVEY_FREE"` - The point position is not pre-defined and is estimated during camera solving.
* |value|`"SURVEY_APPROX"` - The point position is approximately known and is used to guide the camera solving process.
* |value|`"SURVEY_EXACT"` - The point position is exactly known and is used as a fixed reference during camera solving.
* |value|`"SURVEY_LINEUP"` - The point has a 3D position and is used for lineup purposes; it is not used for camera solving.
TrackingMode_t: * |value|`"TRACKING_MARKER"` - Track a marker, i.e. an object with two well-defined color zones,
such as a circular marker with a ring in one color and an inner circle in another color.
Other shapes with similar color zoning can also be tracked as markers, as long as it is possible
to estimate a center position based on the color zoning. Image_controls can be used to enhance
the color zoning of markers and improve tracking stability.
WidgetAttachMode_t: This type is used by multiple functions, e.g. |func|`tde4.createCustomRequester`, |func|`tde4.setWidgetLinks`, and |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets`.
It describes how a widget is attached to its parent widget, window, or another widget.
Offsets and percentage positions are interpreted in |term|`GUI_coordinates`. Possible values are:
* |value|`"ATTACH_WIDGET"` - Attach to the specified widget. |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets` expects an offset in pixels from the specified widget.
* |value|`"ATTACH_OPPOSITE_WIDGET"` - Attach to the opposite side of the specified widget. |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets` expects an offset in pixels from the opposite side of the specified widget.
* |value|`"ATTACH_WINDOW"` - Attach to the parent window. |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets` expects an offset in pixels from the parent window.
* |value|`"ATTACH_OPPOSITE_WINDOW"` - Attach to the opposite side of the parent window. |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets` expects an offset in pixels from the opposite side of the parent window.
* |value|`"ATTACH_POSITION"` - Specify the position in percentage relative to the parent.
If this mode is used, the position is determined by the offsets set by |func|`tde4.setWidgetOffsets`
and is interpreted as percentage of the parent widget or window's size along the corresponding GUI axis, rather than absolute pixel offsets.
* |value|`"ATTACH_NONE"` - Do not attach. Missing positions are computed based on the current position and default size of the widget.
* |value|`"ATTACH_AS_IS"` - Keep the current attachment. This allows to modify some of the widget's attachments while keeping the others unchanged.
WidgetLabelAlign_t: This type defines horizontal text alignment for label widgets.
* |value|`"ALIGN_LABEL_LEFT"` - Align label text to the left.
* |value|`"ALIGN_LABEL_RIGHT"` - Align label text to the right.
WidgetCallbackFunction_t: |type|`Callable[[RequesterID_t, WidgetName_t, WidgetCallbackActionEnum], None]` - Standard widget callback function type. The callback receives the requester ID, widget name,
and a high-level widget action code.
WidgetRawEventCallbackFunction_t: |type|`Callable[[RequesterID_t, WidgetName_t, int, int, WidgetCallbackRawEventEnum, WidgetCallbackModKeysEnum, int], None]` - Raw event callback function type for widgets. The callback receives the requester ID, widget name, mouse position |var|`(x, y)`,
raw event type, modifier keys, and an event-dependent raw payload value.

doc_lines: |value|`0` - Generic activation/click action used by many widgets (e.g. button, menu, option-menu, toggle, tabs).
|value|`1` - Double-click action for widgets that support double-click interaction (e.g. list, textarea, some menu/file-list paths).
|value|`2` - Widget value changed (used by editable/value widgets such as textfield, textarea, scale, slider, scroll widgets, and curve-area value edits).
|value|`3` - Drag/update-in-progress action during pointer dragging (e.g. list drag, pane drag, scale/slider/scroll drag, curve-area drag).
|value|`4` - Collapse/expand action for hierarchical list/tree style widgets.
|value|`5` - Pre-display hook action fired immediately before display of certain widgets (used by context menu pre-display callbacks).
|value|`6` - Transform operation action (observed in curve-area transform interactions).
|value|`7` - Mouse-down action emitted before button release (used by up/down-action button mode and shortcut-triggered button handling).
|value|`8` - Selection action (observed in curve-area selection handling).
|value|`12` - Deselection action (observed in curve-area deselection handling).
|value|`13` - Alternative value-changed action (observed in textarea-specific edit flows).
|value|`14` - Second alternative value-changed action (observed in textarea-specific extended edit flows).
|value|`15` - Focus-loss action (observed in textarea focus transitions).

general: MUST represent action codes passed to standard widget callbacks.

CLICK: |value|`0` - Generic activation/click action used by many widgets (e.g. button, menu, option-menu, toggle, tabs).
DCLICK: |value|`1` - Double-click action for widgets that support double-click interaction (e.g. list, textarea, some menu/file-list paths).
VALUE_CHANGED: |value|`2` - Widget value changed (used by editable/value widgets such as textfield, textarea, scale, slider, scroll widgets, and curve-area value edits).
DRAG: |value|`3` - Drag/update-in-progress action during pointer dragging (e.g. list drag, pane drag, scale/slider/scroll drag, curve-area drag).
COLLAPSE: |value|`4` - Collapse/expand action for hierarchical list/tree style widgets.
PREDISPLAY_HOOK: |value|`5` - Pre-display hook action fired immediately before display of certain widgets (used by context menu pre-display callbacks).
TRANSFORM: |value|`6` - Transform operation action (observed in curve-area transform interactions).
DOWN_CLICK: |value|`7` - Mouse-down action emitted before button release (used by up/down-action button mode and shortcut-triggered button handling).
SELECTION: |value|`8` - Selection action (observed in curve-area selection handling).
DESELECTION: |value|`12` - Deselection action (observed in curve-area deselection handling).
VALUE_CHANGED_ALT: |value|`13` - Alternative value-changed action (observed in textarea-specific edit flows).
VALUE_CHANGED_ALT2: |value|`14` - Second alternative value-changed action (observed in textarea-specific extended edit flows).
LOOSE_FOCUS: |value|`15` - Focus-loss action (observed in textarea focus transitions).

Scope: Action semantics are widget-dependent; not every widget emits every action.

doc_lines: |value|`0` - A mouse button was pressed down.
|value|`4` - The mouse was moved without any buttons pressed.

general: MUST represent the type of raw event that triggered a raw-event widget callback.

BUTTON_DOWN: |value|`0` - A mouse button was pressed down.
PASSIVE_MOTION: |value|`4` - The mouse was moved without any buttons pressed.

Raw_data: The raw callback payload parameter |var|`data` is event-dependent:
for button events it carries button codes, for keypress events it carries key codes,
for wheel events it carries wheel direction; for motion events it may be |value|`0`.

general: MUST represent modifier keys that were pressed during a raw event that triggered a raw-event widget callback.
Numeric values MUST allow bitwise combination to represent multiple modifier keys pressed at the same time.
constructor: Derived from |type|`IntEnum`. Modifier bit values passed to raw-event widget callbacks to indicate which modifier keys were pressed during the raw event that triggered the callback.

Purpose: The modifier key values can be used in raw-event callbacks to determine which modifier keys were pressed during the user interaction that triggered the callback, allowing to implement different behaviors based on the combination of raw event type and modifier keys.

general: MUST represent specific codes for raw event data passed to raw-event widget callbacks, for certain types of raw events. These codes are used in the |var|`data` parameter of raw-event callbacks to provide additional information about the raw event that triggered the callback, such as which mouse button was pressed
constructor: Derived from |type|`IntEnum`. Specific codes for raw event data passed to raw-event widget callbacks, for certain types of raw events.

general: MUST represent key codes for keyboard shortcuts that can trigger widget callbacks when the user
presses the corresponding key combinations while a widget has focus.

Purpose: Key codes for keyboard shortcuts that can trigger widget callbacks when the user presses
the corresponding key combinations while a widget has focus.
The numeric values correspond to key codes sent by the underlying GUI framework and can be
used directly in callback implementations to identify the triggering key combination.

general: MUST return the installation path of 3DE.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.

general: MUST return the version of 3DE.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.

general: MUST remove the lens distortion from the given 2D vector according to the camera's lens parameters and distortion model for the specified frame.

camera_id: The ID of the camera.
vector_2d: The 2D vector from which to remove distortion, in |term|`Image_units`.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MAY raise if validation fails.
RuntimeError: MUST raise if camera object is not linked to a lens object.

general: MUST apply the lens distortion to the given 2D vector according to the camera's lens parameters and distortion model for the specified frame.

camera_id: The ID of the camera.
vector_2d: The 2D vector to which to apply distortion, in |term|`Image_units`.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MAY raise if validation fails.
RuntimeError: MUST raise if camera object is not linked to a lens object.

Description: The function estimates a homography that transforms the source point set |var|`p`
into the target point set |var|`q` using a least-squares method.
The modes do not represent all possible combinations of translation,
scaling, and rotation. While modes containing |lit|`T` without |lit|`Y` are
general purpose, the |lit|`TY` modes have been implemented particularly for
"consolidation" in stereoscopy, e.g. align the right eye image with respect
to the left eye image, however in y-direction only.

general: MUST compute a 2D homography from two sets of 2D points.
The computation MUST be performed on point sets shifted by the given pivot.
requires: |var|`p`, |var|`q`, and |var|`w` MUST have the same size.
Depending on |var|`mode`, there MUST be between one and four points in |var|`p` (and hence in |var|`q`).
At least a subset of the minimum required number of points SHOULD be in general position (i.e. no three points may be collinear).
The value of |var|`mode` MUST be one of the terms defined in section |label|`Definitions`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_TY"` there MUST be at least one point in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_STY"` there MUST be at least two points in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_RTY"` there MUST be at least two points in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_SRTY"` there MUST be at least two points in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_SRTYP"` there MUST be at least three points in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_T"` there MUST be at least one point in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_ST"` there MUST be at least two points in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_RT"` there MUST be at least two points in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_SRT"` there MUST be at least two points in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_SRTP"` there MUST be at least three points in |var|`p`.
For mode |value|`"HOM_HOM"` there MUST be at least four points in |var|`p`.
ensures: The returned matrix |var|`m` transforms each point in |var|`p` into the corresponding point in |var|`q`, up to numeric precision limits, with respect to the pivot: |lit|`q = m * (p - pivot) + pivot` where |op|`*` denotes the application of the homography to a 2D vector as defined in projective geometry.

w: Weight factors associated with the point correspondences.
pivot: The pivot. |var|`p` and |var|`q` are shifted by this pivot. The homography is estimated for the shifted point sets.
mode: The mode that describes the subset of allowed homographies,

Review status: To be reviewed; currently reported to crash under 3DE4 8.1 in practical testing.
Known issue: In 3DE4 8.1, mode |value|`"MONOMIAL"` is reported to behave like |value|`"CHEBYSHEV1"` (missing dedicated monomial implementation).
Numerics: For higher polynomial degrees, |value|`"CHEBYSHEV1"` is typically numerically more stable than monomial bases.

general: MUST post a custom requester as a non-modal dialog and continue script execution immediately.
requires: The custom requester |var|`requester_id` MUST be created with |func|`tde4.createCustomRequester`.

requester_id: Handle returned by |func|`tde4.createCustomRequester()` identifying the dialog.
window_title: Title shown in the posted requester window or container.
width: Requested window/container width in pixels. If |value|`<= 0`, an internal default width is used.
height: Requested window/container height in pixels. If |value|`<= 0`, an internal height is computed from widget layout.
update_callback_function_name: Optional callback function name for coarse requester-update notifications.
If provided, the callback MUST be invoked once when the requester is posted and MAY be invoked again whenever 3DE4 detects a main-program change that can require a requester refresh.
The callback signal is change-only (no detailed delta payload); callback logic SHOULD re-query current state (for example point lists) and rebuild affected widgets.

Best-effort cleanup pattern: Maintain a dictionary keyed by requester ids and run a sweep inside |var|`update_callback_function_name` that removes ids whose state from |func|`tde4.isCustomRequesterPosted` equals |value|`"REQUESTER_UNPOSTED"`.
This is callback-driven and therefore best-effort; without a dedicated unpost hook, the final closed requester may remain cached until a later callback cycle.

general: MUST execute 3DE4's main core calculation pass for the current project state.
ensures: The return value MUST be an integer success flag (|value|`1` success, |value|`0` failure).
|var|`update` is interpreted as integer boolean with special handling:
|value|`1` enables GUI/event updates; any other value disables GUI/event updates.
The global calculation counter MUST be incremented once per call.
If the solve succeeds, deviation values for points in mode |value|`DBP_POINT_OFF` are reset to |value|`0.0`.

update: If |value|`1`, GUI refresh and event flush are performed after solving.

Returns: |value|`1` if the core solve succeeded, otherwise |value|`0`.

Scope: This is the central project solve entry point used by the application.
It updates camera/pgroup motion paths and 3D point results.
Pipeline_context: In typical 3DE4 workflows, this pass includes postfilter application, interpolation of missing per-frame data,
and extraction of group motion for mocap point groups.
Out_of_scope: Camera and lens calibration parameter estimation is not solved by this call directly.
Those are handled by dedicated tools that may invoke this core solve as part of broader optimization workflows.

general: MUST return the current internal solver calculation counter.

TypeError: MUST raise if unexpected arguments are provided.

text: A parameter name refers to a |dfn|`Valid_Parameter` if it is: |var|`"Focal Length"`, |var|`"Lens Center Offset X"`, |var|`"Lens Center Offset Y"`, |var|`"Pixel Aspect"`, or one of the parameters specific to the lens distortion model of the lens linked to the given camera. The lens distortion models and their parameters are explained in Detail in the documention of the LDPK: |ref|`LDPK <https://3dequalizer.com/?site=tech_docs&id=110216_01>`. Use functions mentioned in |label|`See_also` in order to iterate over lens distortion parameters.

Matrix Points: The points placed by means of 3DE4's matrix tool in |label|`Distortion Grid` view.

general: MUST use the lens distortion Matrix Points assigned to the given camera and estimate the specified lens distortion parameters.

MUST extract all parameters required for computation from the given camera including Matrix Point positions, Matrix Point enabled flag, field of view (which defines the coordinate system), pre-defined camera and lens parameters.
MUST use pre-defined camera and lens distortion parameters as initial values
for the underlying iterative procedures.
The result MUST be stored in the lens object linked to the camera according to the following conditions:
If lens distortion is static the function MUST store the results in the lens object linked to the camera.
If lens distortion is dynamic and driven by either focal length or custom focus distance, MUST store the results in the Lens Distortion Curves for each lens distortion parameter.
If lens distortion is dynamic and driven by focal length and custom focus distance, MUST store the results in the 2D-LUTs for each lens distortion parameter.
requires: There MUST be a "sufficiently large" number of Matrix Points.
The Matrix Points MUST be distributed over a "large area" in the camera's Field of View.

\"large\": The normative description is incomplete due to the complexity of the underling numerics.
Typically there should be as many Matrix Points as can be extracted from the grid shot,

signature.text: calcCameraLSF(camera_pos_3d: 'Vector3D_t', camera_rot_3d: 'Vector3D_t', fback_w: 'float', fback_h: 'float', fov_w_px: 'float', fov_h_px: 'float', lco_x: 'float', lco_y: 'float', focal: 'float', points: 'list[list[Vector3D_t | Vector2D_t | float]]', lock_px: 'int', lock_py: 'int', lock_pz: 'int', lock_rx: 'int', lock_ry: 'int', lock_rz: 'int', lock_focal: 'int', calc_from_scratch: 'int | None' = None) -> 'LSFResult_t'

general: MUST solve camera position, camera rotation, and focal length for one frame from weighted 3D-2D correspondences using the single-frame LSF solver.
The solve MUST use lock flags for translation, rotation, and focal length exactly as provided.
ensures: If the internal solver fails, the returned position and rotation MUST match the input |var|`camera_pos_3d` and |var|`camera_rot_3d`, and deviation MUST be |value|`-1.0`.

camera_rot_3d: Initial camera rotation as Euler angles in |term|`Rotation_order_ZXY`, in degrees.
lco_x: Lens center offset x in cm.
lco_y: Lens center offset y in cm.
points: List of point observations, each as |value|`[point_pos_3d, point_pos_2d, weight]`.
Each entry MUST contain exactly three elements.
|var|`point_pos_3d` MUST be a 3D vector, |var|`point_pos_2d` MUST be a 2D vector, and |var|`weight` MUST be a float.
At least one point entry MUST be provided.
calc_from_scratch: Initial mode flag passed to the solver. |value|`1` starts from scratch, |value|`0` refines from the supplied initial values.

Returns: MUST return a list |value|`[position_3d, rotation_3d, focal, deviation_px]` where:
* |var|`position_3d` is the estimated camera position as a 3-element list of |type|`float`.
* |var|`rotation_3d` is the estimated camera rotation as a 3-element list of |type|`float` in Euler degrees (|term|`Rotation_order_ZXY`).
* |var|`focal` is the estimated focal length in cm.
* |var|`deviation_px` is the estimated reprojection deviation in px.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
RuntimeError: MUST raise if |var|`points` is not a list.
MUST raise if |var|`points` is empty.
MUST raise if an element of |var|`points` is not a list.
MUST raise if an element of |var|`points` does not contain exactly three elements.
MUST raise if element 3 (weight) of an observation is not a float.

Units: The API combines cm (filmback/lens center/focal), px (FOV size, deviation), and Euler angles in |term|`Rotation_order_ZXY` expressed in degrees.
Workflow: Common usage for camera-curve consistency is: collect undistorted 2D observations, run |func|`tde4.calcCameraLSF`, apply the returned rotation/position, then call |func|`tde4.filterPGroup`.

signature.text: calcObjectLSF(object_pos_3d: 'Vector3D_t', object_rot_3d: 'Vector3D_t', camera_pos_3d: 'Vector3D_t', camera_rot_3d: 'Vector3D_t', fback_w: 'float', fback_h: 'float', fov_w_px: 'float', fov_h_px: 'float', lco_x: 'float', lco_y: 'float', focal: 'float', points: 'list[list[Vector3D_t | Vector2D_t | float]]', lock_px: 'int', lock_py: 'int', lock_pz: 'int', lock_rx: 'int', lock_ry: 'int', lock_rz: 'int', lock_focal: 'int', calc_from_scratch: 'int | None' = None) -> 'LSFResult_t'

general: MUST solve object-point-group position, object-point-group rotation, and focal length for one frame from weighted 3D-2D correspondences using the single-frame LSF solver.
Object and camera rotations MUST be interpreted in Euler degrees with |term|`Rotation_order_ZXY`.
Object pose inputs and outputs MUST be interpreted in global-space representation.
The solve MUST use lock flags for translation, rotation, and focal length exactly as provided.
ensures: If the internal solver fails, the returned position and rotation MUST match the input |var|`object_pos_3d` and |var|`object_rot_3d`, and deviation MUST be |value|`-1.0`.

object_pos_3d: Initial object-point-group position in global-space representation.
object_rot_3d: Initial object-point-group rotation in global-space representation, as Euler degrees in |term|`Rotation_order_ZXY`.
camera_pos_3d: Camera position for the solve frame (global coordinates).
camera_rot_3d: Camera rotation for the solve frame, as Euler degrees in |term|`Rotation_order_ZXY`.
lco_x: Lens center offset x in cm.
lco_y: Lens center offset y in cm.
points: List of point observations, each as |value|`[point_pos_3d, point_pos_2d, weight]`.
Each entry MUST contain exactly three elements.
|var|`point_pos_3d` MUST be a 3D vector, |var|`point_pos_2d` MUST be a 2D vector, and |var|`weight` MUST be a float.
At least one point entry MUST be provided.
calc_from_scratch: Initial mode flag passed to the solver. |value|`1` starts from scratch, |value|`0` refines from the supplied initial values.

Returns: MUST return a list |value|`[position_3d, rotation_3d, focal, deviation_px]` where:
* |var|`position_3d` is the estimated object-point-group position as a 3-element list of |type|`float` in global-space representation.
* |var|`rotation_3d` is the estimated object-point-group rotation as a 3-element list of |type|`float` in Euler degrees (|term|`Rotation_order_ZXY`), in global-space representation.
* |var|`focal` is the estimated focal length in cm.
* |var|`deviation_px` is the estimated reprojection deviation in px.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
RuntimeError: MUST raise if |var|`points` is not a list.
MUST raise if |var|`points` is empty.
MUST raise if an element of |var|`points` is not a list.
MUST raise if an element of |var|`points` does not contain exactly three elements.
MUST raise if element 3 (weight) of an observation is not a float.

Units: The API combines cm (filmback/lens center/focal), px (FOV size, deviation), and Euler angles in |term|`Rotation_order_ZXY` expressed in degrees.
Workflow: For object/mocap point groups, a common workflow is: evaluate global-space edit curves, run |func|`tde4.calcObjectLSF`, write results to global-space curves, then call |func|`tde4.makePGroupChildSpaceCurvesConsistentToGlobalSpaceCurves` and |func|`tde4.copyPGroupEditCurvesToFilteredCurves`.

requires: |var|`preset` MUST be in range |value|`[1, 10]`.
ensures: The given contrast value is clamped to |value|`[0.0, 1.0]` before storage.
Lower contrast values MUST produce softer mask transitions.
Higher contrast values MUST produce harder mask transitions.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MUST raise if |var|`preset` is outside range |value|`[1, 10]`.

Mask interpretation: Unmarked regions correspond to mask value |value|`0`; marked regions correspond to mask value |value|`1`.
At contrast |value|`0`, mask values are smoothly interpolated within the sampled color region.
At contrast |value|`1`, the mask is close to binary but still retains a small transition region.

requires: |var|`preset` MUST be in range |value|`[1, 10]`.
ensures: |var|`blur_sharpen` MUST be clamped to integer range |value|`[-6, 6]` before storage.
Negative values represent blur, positive values represent sharpening, and |value|`0` is neutral.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MUST raise if |var|`preset` is outside range |value|`[1, 10]`.

Pipeline context: Blur/Sharpen is a separate stage from historical High Pass processing in the GUI pipeline.
Blur levels: Blur values map as follows: |value|`0` no blur, |value|`-1` 3x3 Gaussian, |value|`-2` 5x5 Gaussian, |value|`-3` 7x7 Gaussian, |value|`-4` 9x9 Gaussian, |value|`-5` 11x11 Gaussian, |value|`-6` 13x13 Gaussian.
Implementation uses a binomial filter; Gaussian naming is used for practical interpretation.

Blur levels: Blur values map as follows: |value|`0` no blur, |value|`-1` 3x3 Gaussian, |value|`-2` 5x5 Gaussian, |value|`-3` 7x7 Gaussian, |value|`-4` 9x9 Gaussian, |value|`-5` 11x11 Gaussian, |value|`-6` 13x13 Gaussian.
Implementation uses a binomial filter; Gaussian naming is used for practical interpretation.

When camera motion changes over time, this global-space representation can become strongly frame-dependent.

general: MUST set the frame edit position of the point group for the given camera and frame.
For object and mocap point groups, this operation MUST set the position of the point group in the coordinate system of |var|`camera_id` for the given frame.
For camera point groups, this operation MUST set the camera position for the given frame.
Callers MUST NOT assume this function is a direct inverse of |func|`tde4.getPGroupPosition3D`; getter values are obtained through type-specific filtered/scaled paths.

pgroup_id: The point group whose frame edit position is updated.
camera_id: Reference camera that defines the transform context.
frame: Frame index where the position is updated.
position: For object and mocap point groups, |var|`position` represents the point-group position in the coordinate system of |var|`camera_id`.
For the camera point group, |var|`position` represents the camera position in world coordinates.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MUST raise if the given point group ID does not refer to an existing point group.
MUST raise if the given camera ID does not refer to an existing camera.
MUST raise if |var|`frame` is outside the valid frame range of the given camera.

Consistency: Modifying the position of a point group or the camera usually breaks the consistency of a solve.
To reduce inconsistency introduced by such operations, the LSF solver may be used: a change in position is compensated by adjusting rotation.
The GUI provides a function named "|label|`Curve Editor`->|label|`Make Rotation Curves Consistent`" that applies
this compensation to all frames of a point group / camera pair.
Global_space_relationship: For object point groups, this setter writes edit-transform data, but global-space edit curves depend on the combined effect of camera motion, object scale, and object transform.
In particular, with non-unit object scale and moving camera, global-space position curves are not a direct frame-by-frame echo of |var|`position`.
Todo: Detailed explanation on the LSF-solver compensation mechanism, and its limitations.

general: MUST set the frame edit rotation of the point group for the given camera and frame.
For object and mocap point groups, this operation MUST set the rotation of the point group in the coordinate system of |var|`camera_id` for the given frame.
For camera point groups, this operation MUST set the camera rotation for the given frame.
Callers MUST NOT assume this function is a direct inverse of |func|`tde4.getPGroupRotation3D`; getter values are obtained through type-specific filtered/scaled paths.

pgroup_id: The point group whose frame edit rotation is updated.
camera_id: Reference camera that defines the transform context.
frame: Frame index where the rotation is updated.
rotation: For object and mocap point groups, |var|`rotation` represents the point-group rotation in the coordinate system of |var|`camera_id`.
For the camera point group, |var|`rotation` represents the camera rotation in world coordinates.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MUST raise if the given point group ID does not refer to an existing point group.
MUST raise if the given camera ID does not refer to an existing camera.

Consistency: Modifying the rotation of a point group or the camera usually breaks the consistency of a solve.
To reduce inconsistency introduced by such operations, the LSF solver may be used: a change in rotation is compensated by adjusting position.
The GUI provides a function named "|label|`Curve Editor`->|label|`Make Position Curves Consistent`" that applies
this compensation to all frames of a point group / camera pair.
Global_space_relationship: For object point groups, this setter writes edit-transform data, but global-space rotation curves depend on the composed transform chain that also includes camera motion and object scale.
Therefore, with non-unit object scale and moving camera, global-space Euler channels should be interpreted as a derived representation, not as direct storage of |var|`rotation`.
Todo: Detailed explanation on the LSF-solver compensation mechanism, and its limitations.

general: MUST execute the point-group filtering step for one point-group/camera pair.
The operation transforms |term|`Edit_curve` data into |term|`Filtered_curve` data according to current postfilter settings.
ensures: If postfilter mode is |value|`"POSTFILTER_OFF"` or camera position constraints are channel-lock based, the operation MUST copy |term|`Edit_curve` data to |term|`Filtered_curve` data (with core-side interpolation/copy behavior) instead of applying smoothing.
The operation MUST respect active camera constraints.

pgroup_id: The point group to filter.
camera_id: The camera of the point-group/camera pair.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MUST raise if |var|`pgroup_id` is not a valid point group.

GUI_visibility: Edits to |term|`Edit_curve` are typically reflected in GUI motion display after this filter step updates |term|`Filtered_curve`.
To inspect edits one-to-one, postfiltering can be disabled.

|func|`tde4.getCameraSelectionFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.

|func|`tde4.getCameraFPS` MUST return |value|`24.0`.

|func|`tde4.getCameraFrameOffset` MUST return |value|`1`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraWeight` MUST return |value|`1.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraTimeshift` MUST return |value|`0.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraCalculationRange` MUST return |value|`[1, 0]`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraFrameRangeCalculationFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraFocalLengthMode` MUST return |value|`"FOCAL_USE_FROM_LENS"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraZoomingFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraFocusMode` MUST return |value|`"FOCUS_USE_FROM_LENS"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraStereoMode` MUST return |value|`"STEREO_OFF"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraStereoOrientation` MUST return |value|`"STEREO_LEFT"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraStereoInterocular` MUST return |value|`"STEREO_IO_STATIC"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraStereoStaticInterocular` MUST return |value|`6.5`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraStereoStaticVerticalShift` MUST return |value|`0.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraStereoStaticDepthShift` MUST return |value|`0.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraStereoRotationPolicy` MUST return |value|`1`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraRollingShutterEnabledFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraRollingShutterTimeShift` MUST return |value|`0.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraRollingShutterContentDistance` MUST return |value|`100.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCamera8BitColorBlackWhite` MUST return |value|`[0.0, 0.0]`.
|func|`tde4.getCamera8BitColorGamma` MUST return |value|`1.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCamera8BitColorSoftclip` MUST return |value|`0.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraSyncMode` MUST return |value|`"SYNC_OFF"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraSyncConstraints` MUST return |value|`"SYNC_CONSTR_NONE"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraSyncTimeshift` MUST return |value|`0.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraPosConstraintMode` MUST return |value|`"PCONSTR_NONE"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraPosConstraintOrientation` MUST return |value|`"PCONSTR_ORIENT_NONE"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraPosConstraintForceY` MUST return |value|`[0, 0.0]`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraPosRotLockChannelFlags` MUST return |value|`[0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraLongLatConversionEnabledFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraLongLatConversionResolution` MUST return |value|`[2048, 2048]`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraLongLatConversionHorizAngle` MUST return |value|`90.0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraMasksEnabledFlag` MUST return |value|`1`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraMasksInvertedFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraFOVMask` MUST return |value|`[0.0, 1.0, 0.0, 1.0]`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraImportEXRDisplayWindowFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraImportSXRRightEyeFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraImageMaskEnabledFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraImageMaskInvertedFlag` MUST return |value|`0`.

|func|`tde4.getCameraImageMaskType` MUST return |value|`"NONINTERLACE"`.

|func|`tde4.getCameraType` MUST return |value|`"SEQUENCE"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraNoFrames` MUST return |value|`0`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraPlaybackRange` MUST return |value|`[0, 0]`.
If |var|`mode` is |value|`"REF_FRAME"`:
|func|`tde4.getCameraType` MUST return |value|`"REF_FRAME"`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraNoFrames` MUST return |value|`1`.
|func|`tde4.getCameraPlaybackRange` MUST return |value|`[1, 1]`.

general: MUST set the playback frame range of the given camera.
requires: |var|`camera_id` MUST refer to an existing camera.
|var|`start` and |var|`end` SHOULD be valid frame indices for |var|`camera_id`, i.e. greater than or equal to |value|`1` and less than or equal to the number of frames of the camera.
|var|`start` SHOULD be less than or equal to |var|`end`.
ensures: The stored playback range MUST satisfy |var|`start` <= |var|`end`.

Order: If |var|`start` is greater than |var|`end`, both values are swapped internally.

camera_id: The camera whose playback range is to be set.
start: The first frame index of the range argument.
end: The second frame index of the range argument.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MUST raise if the given camera ID does not refer to an existing camera.
MAY raise if |var|`start` or |var|`end` is out of range for the given camera.
UserWarning: MAY issue if the camera has no frames.

general: MUST return the playback frame range of the given camera.

camera_id: The camera for which to get the playback frame range.

Returns: * |var|`start` is the first frame of the playback range.
* |var|`end` is the last frame of the playback range.

Usage: This mapping is primarily relevant when displaying higher-precision image data (for example floating-point EXR channels) through an 8-bit display transform.
The resulting 8-bit image representation is also the image basis used by the tracker after Image Controls are applied.

ValueError: MUST raise if the given camera ID does not refer to an existing camera.

Interpretation: The gamma parameter controls the non-linear display shaping used for monitor output.
The transformed 8-bit output is also used as tracking input after Image Controls are applied.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MUST raise if the given camera ID does not refer to an existing camera.

Interpretation: |value|`0.0` corresponds to hard clipping in highlights.
Values greater than |value|`0.0` apply progressively softer highlight roll-off toward white.
The transformed 8-bit output is also used as tracking input after Image Controls are applied.

general: MUST set the 8-bit softclip parameter of the given camera.
MUST clamp |var|`softclip` to the interval |value|`[0.0, 1.0]`.

camera_id: The camera whose 8-bit softclip parameter is set.

TypeError: MUST raise if the given arguments do not match the required types/signature.
ValueError: MUST raise if the given camera ID does not refer to an existing camera.

Interpretation: Higher values shift a larger portion of bright values from pure gamma mapping into soft highlight compression.
The transformed 8-bit output is also used as tracking input after Image Controls are applied.

general: MUST return the ID of the next point in the given point group after the given point, or None if the given point is the last point in the point group.
If use_gui_order is non-zero (|lit|`!= 0`), MUST return the next point according to the current GUI order of the points in the point group.
Otherwise (0 or |None|), MUST return the next point according to the internal storage order of the points in the point group.
requires: The point group with the given point group ID MUST exist.
The given point ID MUST refer to an existing point in the given point group.

Returns: The ID of the next point in the given point group after the given point, or |None| if the given point is the last point in the point group.
